title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhed: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

Trump faces pressure to escalate in Iran after deaths of two US soldiers
subhed: The resumption of strikes and other acts of intimidation between Americans and Iranians on July 7 has undermined the protocol agreement signed in June. The US president, searching for a strategy, appears at a loss as the conflict drags on.

title: 'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
subhed: The excessive commercialization and blatant conflicts of interest that have characterized the 2026 World Cup should convince governments, athletes and other sports stakeholders to take back control, argue researchers Frédérique Reynertz and Pim Verschuuren.

title: BTS electrifies Stade de France, seven years after their last Paris concert
subhed: The world tour of the industry's most powerful K-pop group wrapped up its European leg in Saint-Denis on July 17 and July 18.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

subhed: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'

title: After three heatwaves, French people worry: 'Next year will be worse. How will we cope?'
subhed: Across France, people of all ages have realized how vulnerable they are to extreme temperatures, which have stunned the country during these past weeks.

title: Frontex continuing mission in Greece despite serious violations of migrants' rights
subhed: How long will Frontex tolerate the growing violations of migrants' rights in Greece? A Le Monde investigation documents the many warnings issued by the Fundamental Rights Office of the European agency, which have so far failed to prompt meaningful action.
